summary: Arrival Day — landed EWR, Uber to Home2 Suites
text: The morning light filtered through latticed windows, casting geometric shadows on the stone floor. We stood there for what felt like hours, though it might have been minutes. Some places slow time down — this was one of them. Ishrat reached for my hand without looking.

summary: Settle In & Planning — Baithakh, The Pho House
text: Streets narrowed into alleys that smelled of cardamom and woodsmoke. A vendor called out something we didn’t understand, but the warmth in his voice needed no translation. She stopped at every stall, touching fabrics, holding ceramics up to the light. I carried the bags and watched her come alive.

The water stretched out before us, impossibly blue, the kind of blue that makes you question every other blue you’ve ever seen. She leaned against the railing and said nothing. I said nothing. The silence between us was the fullest conversation we’d had all week.

summary: Shalwar Kameez Shopping — Rangreza, East Avenue, J. Junaid Jamshed
text: We got lost. Properly, beautifully lost. No map, no signal, just the two of us following our feet through a city that rewarded wandering. Every wrong turn revealed a courtyard, a fountain, a doorway that had been standing for centuries.

summary: The Engagement Party — Fresh Meadows, Queens
text: She went in alone. I sat on the steps outside, watching pigeons and tourists and the way the light changed as clouds moved across the sky. When she came back out, her eyes were different — softer, maybe. Or just more present.

summary: Tulips & Farm Market — Holland Ridge Farms, Eastmont Orchards
text: The table was small, the candles were real, and the menu was in a language neither of us spoke. We pointed at things and hoped. Everything that arrived was extraordinary. She laughed when the dessert came — it was enormous, ridiculous, perfect.

summary: Delaware Day Trip — Longwood Gardens, Brandywine Valley Drive, Christiana Mall
text: The air changed as we climbed. Cooler, thinner, carrying the scent of wild herbs and distant rain. Below us the city looked like a toy — all those lives, all those stories, reduced to specks of light. She said she could see our hotel. I pretended I could too.

summary: Spa Day & Farewell — Ocean Place Spa, Baba Grill Dinner
text: Rain came from nowhere, hard and sudden. We ducked into the first open door — a tea house, ancient and warm, the kind of place where the chairs don’t match and the tea is perfect. We stayed two hours. It became the best memory of the trip.

summary: Until We Meet Again — Farewell Brunch, Pack, Drive to EWR
text: Walking the old walls, I told her about the version of myself I’d imagined at twenty. She told me about hers. Neither of us had become those people, and that was fine. Better than fine.
